Minimal jittable class for multi discrete gymnax spaces.

functions.name: luxai_s3.state.generate_perlin_noise_2d
documentation:
Generate a 2D numpy array of perlin noise.

Args:
    shape: The shape of the generated array (tuple of two ints).
        This must be a multple of res.
    res: The number of periods of noise to generate along each
        axis (tuple of two ints). Note shape must be a multiple of
        res.
    tileable: If the noise should be tileable along each axis
        (tuple of two bools). Defaults to (False, False).
    interpolant: The interpolation function, defaults to
        t*t*t*(t*(t*6 - 15) + 10).

Returns:
    A numpy array of shape shape with the generated noise.

Raises:
    ValueError: If shape is not a multiple of res.

Units in the environment with shape (T, N, 3) for T teams, N max units, and 3 features.

3 features are for position (x, y), and energy

2 features are for position (x, y)

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.state.EnvState.energy_node_fns
documentation:
Energy node functions for computing the energy field of the map. They describe the function with a sequence of numbers

The first number is the function used. The subsequent numbers parameterize the function. The function is applied to distance of map tile to energy node and the function parameters.

documentation: The unit sap cost is the amount of energy a unit uses when it saps another unit. Can change between games.

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.unit_sap_range
documentation: The unit sap range is the range of the unit's sap action.

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.unit_sap_dropoff_factor
documentation: The unit sap dropoff factor multiplied by unit_sap_drain

documentation: The unit energy void factor multiplied by unit_energy

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.fog_of_war
documentation: whether there is fog of war or not

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.unit_sensor_range
documentation:
The unit sensor range is the range of the unit's sensor.
Units provide "vision power" over tiles in range, equal to manhattan distance to the unit.

vision power > 0 that team can see the tiles properties

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.nebula_tile_vision_reduction
documentation:
The nebula tile vision reduction is the amount of vision reduction a nebula tile provides.
A tile can be seen if the vision power over it is > 0.

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.nebula_tile_energy_reduction
documentation: amount of energy nebula tiles reduce from a unit

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.nebula_tile_drift_speed
documentation: how fast nebula tiles drift in one of the diagonal directions over time. If positive, flows to the top/right, negative flows to bottom/left

class_variables.name: luxai_s3.params.EnvParams.energy_node_drift_speed
documentation: how fast energy nodes will move around over time

methods.name: luxai_s3.profiler.Profiler.update_csv
documentation:
Update a csv file with the given data (a dict representing a unique identifier of the result row)
and stats. If the file does not exist, it will be created. The update will replace an existing row
if the given data matches the data in the row. If there are multiple matches, only the first match
will be replaced and the rest are deleted
A simple class to help profile/benchmark simulator code

methods.name: luxai_s3.env.LuxAIS3Env.compute_sensor_masks
documentation:
Compute the vision power and sensor mask for both teams

Algorithm:

For each team, generate a integer vision power array over the map.
For each unit in team, add unit sensor range value (its kind of like the units sensing power/depth) to each tile the unit's sensor range
Clamp the vision power array to range [0, unit_sensing_range].

With 2 vision power maps, take the nebula vision mask * nebula vision power and subtract it from the vision power maps.
Now any time the vision power map has value > 0, the team can sense the tile. This forms the sensor mask

classes.name: luxai_runner.logger.Logger
documentation:
A basic logger

verbosity - how verbose the logger is
    0 : Log nothing
    1 : Log errors
    2 : Log warnings
    3 : Log everything

class_variables.name: luxai_runner.cli.ReplayConfig.save_format
documentation: Format of the replay file, can be either "html" or "json". HTML replays are easier to visualize, JSON replays are easier to analyze programmatically. Defaults to the extension of the path passed to --output, or "json" if there is no extension or it is invalid.

class_variables.name: luxai_runner.cli.ReplayConfig.compressed_obs
documentation: Whether to save compressed observations or not. Compressed observations do not contain the full observation at each step. In particular, the map information is stored as the first observation, subsequent observations only store the changes that happened.

class_variables.name: luxai_runner.cli.Args.players
documentation: Paths to player modules. If --tournament is passed as well, you can also pass a folder and we will look through all sub-folders for valid agents with main.py files (only works for python agents at the moment).

class_variables.name: luxai_runner.cli.Args.len
documentation: Max episode length

class_variables.name: luxai_runner.cli.Args.output
documentation: Where to output replays. Default is none and no replay is generated

class_variables.name: luxai_runner.cli.Args.verbose
documentation: Verbose Level (0 = silent, 1 = (game-ending errors, debug logs from agents), 2 = warnings (non-game ending invalid actions), 3 = info (system info, unit collisions) )

class_variables.name: luxai_runner.cli.Args.seed
